disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abrupt sustained episode of inward-curling hands and non-stop facial twitching in a 3-week-old, beginning about an hour ago
trigger_question_examples: What exactly is his body doing?
When did the twitching start and how long did it last?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: About an hour ago his hands suddenly curled inward and his face started twitching non-stop — the first episode went for around 20 minutes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The jitteriness does not settle when the limbs are gently held still and is set off by touch/handling
trigger_question_examples: Does it stop if you gently hold his arm still?
Is it triggered by anything, like touching him?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — holding his arms still doesn't stop it, and touching or handling him seems to set it off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: During a spasm the infant's lips briefly turned blue while he appeared to hold his breath, then self-resolved
trigger_question_examples: Has his colour changed at all — lips or face going blue?
Has his breathing paused or looked different during an episode?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — during one spasm his lips looked a bit blue for a few seconds while he seemed to hold his breath, then it passed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known unrepaired congenital heart defect (parent-stated Tetralogy of Fallot) on cardiac medications
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
Is he on any regular medicines?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was born with a heart condition — they said Tetralogy of Fallot — and he's on heart medicines. It hasn't been repaired yet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known cleft palate, unrepaired, under feeding-team care
trigger_question_examples: Any problems with his mouth or feeding?
Were there other things noted when he was born?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has a cleft in the roof of his mouth — a cleft palate — and the feeding team helps us.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor feeding and general irritability since discharge from the special care nursery
trigger_question_examples: How has he been feeding and settling since coming home?
Has anything been off in the last few days?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Since he came home from the special care nursery he's been grizzly and not feeding well.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Parents observe a small jaw and low-set ears
trigger_question_examples: Have you or the team noticed anything about the shape of his face or ears?
Did anyone mention how his features look when he was born?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, he does have a small jaw and his ears sit a bit low.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He is not on any calcium or vitamin D supplement
trigger_question_examples: Is he on any vitamins or supplements, like vitamin D or calcium?
Apart from his heart medicines, is he taking anything else at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he's only on his heart medicines — nothing like calcium or vitamin D.
